phase_1.audio: Sound level monitoring shows fluctuation from 89.8 dBA to sustained 72.8 dBA. Higher ambient noise detected; concentration requires active focus effort.
phase_1.vitals: Heart rate averaged 82 bpm throughout session (moderately elevated, consistent with active cognitive engagement). Data indicates user was in suboptimal physiological state for concentration.

phase_2.correlations: Positional settling (GPS convergence) diverges from kinetic patterns. Acoustic fluctuation observed.
phase_2.holistic_assessment: Session exemplifies near-optimal conditions: minimal acoustic disturbance (72.8 dB), variable positioning, and strong focus indicators. Duration (442s) sufficient for sustained concentration assessment.

phase_3.recommendations: Modest noise control (e.g., ambient music, quiet background) could improve focus. Invest in ergonomic furniture to reduce fidgeting and increase stationary time.

reason: Concentration score reflects moderate audio conditions (72.8dB), frequent motion, and adequate session length.
